data.title: Barramundi Group Sets Out 2025–2026 Financial Reporting Schedule
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Barramundi Group Ltd. ( (DE:95Z) ) just unveiled an update.

Barramundi Group Ltd. has published its financial calendar for 2025 and 2026, setting out key reporting and governance dates for investors and other stakeholders. The schedule includes half-yearly and annual reports, as well as the annual general meeting, providing clarity on when the market can expect major financial disclosures.

For the 2025 financial year, the half-year report is planned for 16 February 2026, followed by the annual report on 29 May 2026 and the annual general meeting on 30 June 2026. For 2026, the company intends to release its half-year report on 28 August 2026, aligning with continuing obligations to maintain transparency and regular communication with the market.

More about Barramundi Group Ltd.

Barramundi Group Ltd. operates in the aquaculture sector, focusing on the farming and production of barramundi fish for food markets. The company targets consumers and customers seeking sustainably farmed seafood, positioning itself within the broader global seafood and protein supply chain.

data.title: Harima Chemicals to Divest SunPine Stake in Strategic Capital Shift
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Harima Chemicals Group, Inc. ( (JP:4410) ).

Harima Chemicals Group has decided to sell its entire 24.6% stake in Swedish joint venture SunPine AB, held via Lawter Europe BV, to existing shareholder VAROPreem AB, Sweden’s largest fuel refining and sales company, and is now negotiating final contract terms. The divestment is aimed at improving capital efficiency by monetizing policy‑held shares and reallocating capital to growth areas, while Harima expects no disruption to its tall oil rosin procurement or to FY2025 results, reflecting a response to easing rosin demand and a shift in portfolio strategy.

SunPine has supplied Harima with tall oil rosin under long‑term contracts, and these arrangements are expected to remain in place despite the ownership change, underscoring that the move is a financial and strategic rebalancing rather than an operational retreat. The decision follows Preem’s exercise of its preferential right to acquire the shares on terms equivalent to an external investor’s offer, consolidating SunPine’s ownership among Swedish partners as Harima reviews the potential impact on FY2026 earnings and refocuses capital on higher‑growth segments.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:4410) stock is a Hold with a Yen1056.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Harima Chemicals Group, Inc. stock, see the JP:4410 Stock Forecast page.

More about Harima Chemicals Group, Inc.

Harima Chemicals Group, Inc. is a Japanese specialty chemicals manufacturer listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market, with operations that include resin‑based materials and related chemical products. Through its consolidated subsidiary Lawter Europe BV, the group has been involved in tall oil rosin procurement in Europe, leveraging investments in biomass‑derived chemical ventures such as Sweden’s SunPine AB.

Average Trading Volume: 55,915

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen25.79B

Hongkong Land Holdings ( (GB:HKLD) ) has provided an announcement.

Hongkong Land Holdings has repurchased 170,000 of its ordinary shares on 13 February 2026, paying between US$8.52 and US$8.84 per share, with a weighted average price of US$8.5951. The company plans to cancel the repurchased shares, effectively reducing its share count and potentially enhancing earnings per share for remaining investors.

Following the cancellation, the company’s issued share capital stands at 2,153,619,126 ordinary shares, each carrying one vote and with no treasury shares held. This updated share count provides the new denominator for shareholders to assess and report any notifiable changes in their ownership under UK disclosure rules, clarifying voting rights and transparency obligations in the market.

data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Sunwah Kingsway Capital Holdings Limited ( (HK:0188) ) is now available.

Sunwah Kingsway Capital Holdings has further trimmed its stake in autonomous driving company WeRide through on-market sales of American depositary shares on the Nasdaq Stock Market. The group disposed of 146,000 WeRide ADSs between 25 November 2025 and 16 February 2026 at prices ranging from US$7.65 to US$9.95, raising about US$1.27 million before costs and booking an estimated gain of US$0.8 million.

Combined with earlier disposals of 131,000 WeRide ADSs completed between May and November 2025, the transactions crossed the 5% threshold under Hong Kong listing rules and are classified as a discloseable transaction. Management said the sales, executed at prevailing market prices to unidentified independent buyers, allow the group to realize part of its investment, strengthen cash resources for working capital and future investments, and are considered fair, reasonable and in the interests of shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0188) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sunwah Kingsway Capital Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0188 Stock Forecast page.

More about Sunwah Kingsway Capital Holdings Limited

Sunwah Kingsway Capital Holdings Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated financial services group listed in Hong Kong. The group focuses on securities, commodities and futures broking, corporate finance, asset management, money lending, property investment and proprietary investments, serving capital markets and investment clients in the region.

data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Precise Biometrics AB ( (SE:PREC) ) has shared an announcement.

Precise Biometrics reports strong 2025 sales momentum for its cloud-based visitor management solution, Precise Visit by EastCoast, driven by a fully cloud-focused strategy and rapid customer adoption across sectors. New wins span small and mid-sized firms, public sector bodies, and global enterprises such as ASSA Abloy, Smurfit WestRock, Aqua d’or, and Sundbybergs stad, many of which are standardizing Precise Visit across international office networks.

During 2025, the company grew its Precise Visit cloud customer base by more than 50%, with all new customers choosing cloud and existing on-premise clients accelerating migration. The solution is increasingly embedded in smart office environments, integrating visitor flows with meeting room booking, workspace management, and other digital workplace tools, reinforcing Precise Biometrics’ position in secure, modern workplace infrastructure and supporting its broader cloud transformation.

The most recent analyst rating on (SE:PREC) stock is a Hold with a SEK2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Precise Biometrics AB stock, see the SE:PREC Stock Forecast page.

More about Precise Biometrics AB

Precise Biometrics AB is a Sweden-based pioneer in biometrics and cybersecurity, offering fingerprint, face, and palm recognition algorithms alongside turnkey solutions for visitor management and biometric physical access control. The company serves global device, automotive, and enterprise markets through its Digital Identity and Biometric Technologies units, and is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm with offices in Europe, North America, and Asia.

data.title: Jyske Bank Expands Treasury Position Through Ongoing DKK 3 Billion Share Buyback
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Jyske Bank A/S ( (GB:0MGD) ).

Jyske Bank has been conducting a share repurchase programme since 5 February 2026, running until late January 2027, with a total planned buyback of up to DKK 3 billion under EU Safe Harbour rules. In week 7 of 2026, the bank acquired additional shares, bringing total purchases under the programme to 90,239 shares for DKK 86.9 million at an average price of DKK 963.41.

Following these latest transactions, Jyske Bank now holds 3,399,767 treasury shares, equivalent to 5.53% of its share capital, excluding customer investments and trading positions. The growing treasury share position may support capital management objectives and potentially enhance shareholder value through a reduced free float and more flexible balance-sheet management.

More about Jyske Bank A/S

Jyske Bank A/S is a Danish financial institution operating in the banking sector, offering a range of banking and financial services. The bank’s activities include lending, deposit-taking, investment services and other products aimed at retail and corporate clients in its core markets.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Balfour Beatty ( (GB:BBY) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Balfour Beatty has continued executing its previously announced share buyback programme, repurchasing 70,000 ordinary shares on 13 February 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of about 765.75 pence per share. The shares will be held in treasury, reducing the freely traded share count and potentially supporting earnings per share and capital return objectives.

Following this latest transaction, the company has bought back a total of 2,084,039 shares under the programme at an average price of roughly 726.41 pence, with all repurchased shares held in treasury with no voting rights. The resulting total number of voting rights stands at 490,764,545, a figure shareholders can use to assess disclosure thresholds and changes in ownership under UK regulatory rules.

Hill & Smith Holdings ( (GB:HILS) ) has issued an announcement.

Hill & Smith PLC has continued execution of its £100m share buyback programme, repurchasing 10,325 ordinary shares on 13 February 2026 through Deutsche Bank’s London branch at prices between 2,280p and 2,320p, with a volume-weighted average of 2,306.13p. Following cancellation of these shares, the company’s total shares in issue fall to 79,504,514, with 1,295,042 shares cumulatively bought back under the programme, modestly enhancing earnings per share and signalling ongoing capital returns to investors.

The transaction was executed as an on-exchange trade on the London Stock Exchange, in line with market regulations and the parameters of the previously announced buyback. By persistently reducing its share count, Hill & Smith underscores its confidence in the business and balance sheet, while providing incremental support to the share price and improving capital allocation efficiency for shareholders.
